skill.name: yescan-scan-universal
skill.description: Image enhancement skill powered by Quark Scan King. Enhance photo quality, remove handwriting/watermarks/shadows, and apply effects.
skill.summary: AI-powered image enhancement: remove handwriting, watermarks, shadows; HD upscale, crop, and apply sketch effects.
skill.author: yescan-ai

security_audit.summary: The skill is a legitimate image enhancement service that sends user images to a single disclosed API endpoint (scan-business.quark.cn). The vast majority of static findings (607 detected patterns) are false positives caused by the static analyzer misinterpreting Chinese-language documentation, markdown formatting characters, and standard file I/O patterns. The skill accesses one environment variable (SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY) and one configuration file (~/.yescan_env), both properly disclosed. Output files are written to system temp directory. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from backtick characters appearing in markdown inline code formatting. The 'hex-encoded characters' findings are standard image magic-byte detection code. Risk is rated medium due to the nature of sending user images to a third-party API, which is the skill's core function and fully disclosed.

high_findings.title: User images transmitted to third-party API
high_findings.description: The skill sends user-provided images to scan-business.quark.cn for processing. This is the core function of the skill and is fully disclosed in SKILL.md and references/privacy.md. Users must provide an API key to use the service.

medium_findings.title: API key stored in plaintext file
medium_findings.description: The SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY is loaded from ~/.yescan_env if not set as environment variable. This is standard practice for API key configuration and is disclosed in the security documentation. The file should be chmod 600.

medium_findings.title: Output files written to system temp directory
medium_findings.description: Enhanced images are written to /tmp/imgs or equivalent system temp directory. Files are not auto-cleaned. This is disclosed in SECURITY.md.

low_findings.title: Static analysis false positives from Chinese markdown content
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 348 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and numerous 'cryptographic algorithm' and 'Windows SAM database' findings. Upon manual review, these are all false positives: backtick characters in Chinese markdown documentation, and pattern matches on Chinese text that coincidentally contains keyword patterns. The actual code contains no such dangerous patterns.

low_findings.title: Magic byte detection in file_saver.py
low_findings.description: The file format detection code uses hex byte comparisons to identify image formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF, etc.). This is standard image processing code, not obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded API endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: Single hardcoded outbound endpoint scan-business.quark.cn documented in settings.py

content.user_title: Enhance and clean up images with AI
content.value_statement: Photos, screenshots, and scanned documents often contain unwanted elements like handwriting, watermarks, or shadows. This skill uses the Quark Scan King API to automatically enhance image quality, remove visual noise, and apply artistic effects.
actual_capabilities: Remove handwritten notes, watermarks, shadows, and screen patterns from images
Enhance photo quality with HD upscaling and noise reduction
Crop and rectify document images with perspective correction
Apply sketch or line art effects to photos
Enhance exam papers, certificates, and contract images for clarity
Process images via URL, local file path, or base64 data input
limitations: Only processes single static images - no video or batch support
Maximum file size is 5MB per image
Requires a valid SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY API key from Quark Scan King
Images are sent to a third-party API - not suitable for highly sensitive data

use_cases.title: Clean up scanned documents
use_cases.description: Remove handwritten annotations, shadows, and background colors from scanned exam papers, certificates, and contracts to produce clean, printable documents.

use_cases.title: Remove watermarks from images
use_cases.description: Clean watermarks and logos from stock photos or reference images while preserving the original image quality.

use_cases.title: Enhance old or low-quality photos
use_cases.description: Upscale and improve clarity of blurry, dark, or low-resolution photographs for better viewing and sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image enhancement
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance the quality of this image: [image_url_or_path]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick quality improvement for any photo

prompt_templates.title: Remove handwriting from document
prompt_templates.prompt: Clean this scanned exam paper by removing all handwritten notes: [path/to/scan.jpg]
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing clean documents from annotated originals

prompt_templates.title: Remove watermark from image
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the watermark from this stock photo: [image_url]
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning reference images for design work

prompt_templates.title: Apply sketch effect
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this photograph into a sketch drawing: [image_path]

output_examples.input: A photo of an old document with coffee stains and faded text
output: Enhanced image with improved contrast and clarity

output_examples.input: A stock photo with a visible watermark in the corner
output: Clean image with watermark seamlessly removed
No visible artifacts in the cleaned area

best_practices: Always verify the API key is properly configured before processing images
Use chmod 600 on ~/.yescan_env to protect your API key
Clean up output files from /tmp/imgs periodically to manage disk space
Choose the appropriate scene (exam-enhance, remove-watermark, etc.) for best results
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for highly sensitive or confidential documents - images are sent to a third-party API
Do not attempt to process videos or real-time streams - only single static images are supported
Do not provide images larger than 5MB - they will be rejected

faq.question: What API key do I need?
faq.answer: You need a SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY from the Quark Scan King developer platform at https://scan.quark.cn/business. Set it as an environment variable or in ~/.yescan_env.

faq.question: Where are the enhanced images saved?
faq.answer: Output images are written to the system temp directory, typically /tmp/imgs/. You can clean these up manually when no longer needed.

faq.question: Is my data private?
faq.answer: Images are sent to Quark's API for processing. This is documented in the skill's privacy documentation. Do not use for highly sensitive data.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports jpg, jpeg, png, gif, bmp, webp, tiff, and wbmp formats up to 5MB in size.

faq.question: Can I process multiple images at once?
faq.answer: No, the skill processes one image per request. For batch processing, you would need to call the skill multiple times.

faq.question: What if the API returns an error?
faq.answer: Check the error code in the response. Common issues include invalid API key (A0100), quota exceeded (A0211), or invalid scene name. See references/troubleshooting.md for details.
